consistency.level: Consistent with standard D&D 5e spell structure.
consistency.components: Consistent (V, S, M with material component noted).

quality.description_clarity: High. The description clearly outlines size, capacity, behavior when overloaded, movement rules (proximity to caster, terrain limitations), and termination conditions.
quality.mechanics_clarity: High. The rules for movement (immobile within 20ft, follows outside 20ft, hard stops at 100ft distance, 10ft elevation change limit) are specific and easily implementable.

mechanics.core_function: Creates a durable, weight-bearing, mobile platform.
limitations_noted: Weight capacity (500 lbs) leads to termination.
Caster proximity required for control/stability (within 20 ft).
Elevation change restriction (cannot cross 10 ft vertical change).
mechanics.implications: This spell is primarily used for logistics (carrying gear/loot) rather than combat, given its immobile nature when the caster is near and its lack of offensive or defensive capabilities beyond brute-force carrying capacity.

analysis.suggestions: None. The data is complete and accurately reflects the established mechanics for the 'Floating Disk' spell.
